result:
The MVP is built and compiles successfully. Here's the complete deliverable:

---

## 1. Product Concept Summary

**Imagine Worlds** is an AI-assisted 3D creative learning platform where children (under Grade 6) build, explore, and share 3D scenes. An AI companion named "Spark" guides imagination through playful prompts — never replacing creativity, but sparking it. The creation loop is: imagine → build → explore → reflect → iterate.

---

## 2. User Personas

| Persona | Age | Needs | Context |
|---------|-----|-------|---------|
| **Mia the Explorer** | 8 | Wants to build story worlds from books she reads | Home use, tablet |
| **Leo the Builder** | 10 | Loves constructing scenes and experimenting with shapes | Classroom use, desktop |
| **Ms. Park** (Teacher) | 35 | Needs a low-friction creative tool for 30-min class sessions | Classroom facilitator |

---

## 3. Core User Journeys

1. **First Visit**: Landing → Onboarding (name + avatar, <60s) → Builder
2. **Build Session**: Builder → drag objects → change colors/themes → talk to Spark → Save
3. **Explore**: Gallery → pick a world → orbit/walk through it in 3D
4. **Return Visit**: Gallery → load own project → edit and extend

---

## 4. Feature List (MVP Priority)

| Priority | Feature | Status |
|----------|---------|--------|
| P0 | Landing page | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Onboarding (name + avatar) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | 3D World Builder (drag-and-drop, 8 object types) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Environment themes (6 presets) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Color palette (20 colors) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | AI Creative Companion (Spark) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Save/Load projects | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Explore mode (orbit camera) | ✅ Built |
| P0 | Gallery with tabs | ✅ Built |
| P1 | Transform controls (rotate/scale) | Stub ready |
| P1 | Undo/redo | Future |
| P2 | Teacher dashboard | Future |
| P2 | Sharing/community gallery | Future |

---

## 5. Information Architecture

```
/                    → Landing (hero + features + CTA)
/onboarding          → Name + avatar picker
/builder             → 3D workspace + tool palette + properties + AI companion
/explore/[projectId] → Read-only 3D viewer with auto-rotate
/gallery             → My Worlds + Sample Worlds tabs
```

---

## 6. UX/UI Design Direction

- **Color palette**: Purple primary (#6C63FF), pink accent (#FF6B9D), cyan highlight (#00D2FF)
- **Typography**: System font stack, large body text (16px+)
- **Layout**: Full-height builder, floating companion bubble, minimal chrome
- **Interactions**: Big buttons (44px+ targets), drag-and-drop, color swatches
- **Motion**: Hover scale (105%), bounce on landing emoji, smooth transitions
- **Accessibility**: ARIA labels, focus-visible rings, reduced-motion support

---

## 7. Technical Architecture

```
Next.js 15 (App Router, Server + Client Components)
├── React Three Fiber 9 + Drei 10 (3D rendering)
├── Zustand 5 (state: world, child, companion)
├── Prisma 6 + SQLite (persistence)
├── OpenAI API (gpt-4o-mini for companion)
└── Tailwind CSS 4 (styling)
```

- 3D canvas loaded via `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false`
- API routes: `/api/children`, `/api/projects`, `/api/ai/companion`
- State: `useWorldStore` (objects/env), `useChildStore` (persist), `useCompanionStore` (messages)

---

## 8. Database Schema

```
Child (id, name, avatarId, createdAt)
  └── Project[] (id, title, thumbnail, worldData:JSON, childId, isSample, createdAt, updatedAt)
```

---

## 9. AI Prompt System Design

- **Persona**: "Spark" — warm, simple, encouraging creative buddy
- **Rules**: 1-2 sentences max, 7-year-old vocabulary, never critical, child-safe
- **Context sent**: Object count, object types, environment theme, conversation history (last 6 turns)
- **Model**: gpt-4o-mini, temperature 0.8, max_tokens 100
- **Behavior**: Asks "what if" questions, suggests one idea at a time, celebrates creations

---

## 10. Safety & Moderation

- AI system prompt enforces strict content boundaries (no violence, scary, inappropriate content)
- max_tokens capped at 100 to prevent long outputs
- No user-generated content sharing to external services in MVP
- No authentication required (local-first, child privacy)
- No personal data collected beyond first name and avatar choice
- SQLite local database — no cloud data exposure

---

## 11. Implementation Plan (Completed)

All 8 phases delivered: Foundation → Types/Stores → Landing/Onboarding → Builder → Companion → APIs → Explore → Gallery

---

## 12. Project Structure

```
src/
├── app/                          # Routes
│   ├── page.tsx                  # Landing
│   ├── onboarding/page.tsx       # Onboarding
│   ├── builder/page.tsx          # 3D Builder
│   ├── explore/[projectId]/      # Explore mode
│   ├── gallery/page.tsx          # Gallery
│   └── api/                      # API routes
├── components/three/             # Shared 3D components
├── features/
│   ├── builder/components/       # Builder UI (palette, scene, properties)
│   ├── companion/components/     # AI sidebar
│   ├── explore/components/       # Explore scene
│   └── gallery/components/       # Gallery cards
├── stores/                       # Zustand stores
├── types/                        # TypeScript interfaces
└── lib/                          # Prisma, OpenAI, utils, constants
```

---

## 13. Getting Started

```bash
cd /private/tmp/oma-loop-20260506-100111/projects/oma

# Add your OpenAI API key
echo 'OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-your-key"' >> .env.local

# Run dev server
npm run dev

# Seed sample worlds (already done)
npx tsx prisma/seed.ts
```

Then visit `http://localhost:3000`. The full flow works: Landing → Onboarding → Builder (drag objects, change colors/themes, chat with Spark) → Save → Gallery → Explore.
